sections.title: Step 1 — Error Statement Collection
sections.content:
24 needs from Step 06 (JTBD_breweries) + 7 product-related needs from Step 10 (FIT_breweries) = **31 total needs**.

Step 10 entries injected with `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name="product_constraint"`, `impact_category="reliability"`, `product_related=true` per merge rule.

No markets skipped — 31 needs found for Breweries.

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction Recalibration (Incumbent-Grounded)
sections.content:
**Incumbent profiles used for recalibration:**

| Incumbent | Share | Mechanism | Strengths | Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Paddle-wheel / turbine | 50–60% | Rotational (impeller) | Lowest cost ($50–150), simple installation, no fluid config needed | Bearing wear 6–18 mo in glycol, ±2–5% drift, no diagnostics, particle jamming |
| Electromagnetic (mag) | 15–25% | Faraday induction | ±0.5% accuracy, no moving parts, 100:1 turndown, 4-20 mA output | 3–5× cost ($800–2,000), 10–20 W power, larger footprint, requires conductive fluid |
| Manual / do nothing | 15–30% | Visual / thermal probe | Zero cost | No flow visibility, no fault detection, late excursion discovery |

[SRC: JTBD_breweries §2.4 Alternatives, FIT_breweries §11a]

### Recalibration Table — Step 06 Needs

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat_orig | Sat_recal | Recalibration Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Calibration drift <±0.5% / 12mo | 8 | 4 | **3** | PW drifts 2–5% over life (dominant 50–60%); mag stable but minority; manual = zero measurement |
| 2 | Glycol concentration accuracy 25–40% | 8 | 3 | **3** | No incumbent compensates for glycol concentration changes |
| 3 | Full operating range 0.5–50 L/min | 7 | 5 | **5** | Mag handles full range (100:1 turndown); PW limited but functional |
| 4 | Air bubble false readings | 7 | 3 | **3** | PW jams/stalls with air; mag less affected but still errors |
| 5 | Jacket blockage detection <0.5°C | 9 | 2 | **2** | No incumbent provides glycol jacket flow monitoring; manual inspection only |
| 6 | Pump degradation detection >15% | 8 | 3 | **2** | No incumbent trends pump degradation; manual checks only |
| 7 | Process vs noise >95% classification | 8 | 4 | **3** | PW has poor S/N (dominant); mag better but no classification logic |
| 8 | Glycol leak <5% reservoir | 8 | 3 | **2** | No automated leak detection in incumbent brewery glycol setups |
| 9 | Correct flow rate setpoint ±10% | 7 | 4 | **4** | Knowledge/engineering need; sensor-independent |
| 10 | Calculate flow from single config | 6 | 5 | **5** | Knowledge need; sensor-independent |
| 11 | Install without drain | 7 | 3 | **3** | All inline sensors require system drain for installation |
| 12 | Config error >1% from fluid-type | 8 | 5 | **6** | PW needs no fluid config (zero error risk); mag minimal config |
| 13 | Verify accuracy in 5 min pre-batch | 7 | 3 | **3** | PW has no self-diagnostics; mag basic only; mostly skipped in practice |
| 14 | False-normal masking blocked jacket | 9 | 3 | **2** | No incumbent detects partial jacket blockage — completely unaddressed |
| 15 | Isolate anomaly root cause in 5 min | 8 | 3 | **3** | No incumbent provides diagnostic data for root cause isolation |
| 16 | Operator expertise for anomaly data | 7 | 3 | **3** | PW: no diagnostics; mag: complex outputs requiring training |
| 17 | Service life >3yr no maintenance | 7 | 4 | **3** | PW bearing life 6–18 mo in glycol dominates; mag >10yr but minority |
| 18 | Installed cost <$500/point | 8 | 4 | **5** | PW at $50–150 well under target; mag at $800–2,000 exceeds it |
| 19 | No flow path contamination | 6 | 5 | **5** | PW: some debris risk; mag: clean. Mixed |
| 20 | Pressure drop minimization | 6 | 5 | **5** | PW/mag: minimal drop; orifice: high. Mixed |
| 21 | Batch-to-batch temp <±0.5°C | 9 | 4 | **3** | Without per-zone flow monitoring, glycol distribution is uncontrolled |
| 22 | Multi-fermenter without starvation | 8 | 5 | **4** | No per-zone flow visibility → starvation undetected until temp drifts |
| 23 | Quantitative performance data | 7 | 3 | **3** | PW: basic pulse only; mag: better data but too expensive to deploy at every point |
| 24 | Discover excursion before completion | 9 | 3 | **2** | No flow-based early warning exists; relies on slow-response fermenter temp probe |

### Recalibration Table — Step 10 Product-Related Needs

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat_orig | Sat_recal | Recalibration Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PR-1 | Air >2% false readings (ultrasonic) | 7 | 4 | **4** | Mag handles air better; PW jams entirely — mixed |
| PR-2 | Recovery after air entrainment | 6 | 3 | **3** | PW may take minutes to restart after air clears |
| PR-3 | Short straight pipe accuracy | 6 | 4 | **5** | PW is profile-insensitive; mag tolerates shorter runs |
| PR-4 | Measurement blackout partial drain | 5 | 3 | **3** | DP degrades gradually; PW and ultrasonic lose signal entirely |
| PR-5 | Crash-cooling transient error | 6 | 5 | **6** | Mag and PW unaffected by fluid temperature changes to flow reading |
| PR-6 | Low-flow S/N degradation | 5 | 5 | **5** | PW stalls at low flow (worse); mag excellent at low flow |
| PR-7 | Acoustic coupling failure (glycol ratio) | 5 | 4 | **6** | Purely ultrasonic-specific; mag and PW have no acoustic coupling dependency |

---

sections.title: Step 3 — Opportunity Scores (Deterministic)
sections.content:
Formula: `opportunity_score = importance + max(importance - satisfaction, 0)`
Underserved: `importance > 7 AND satisfaction < 5`
Overserved: `importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7`

### Step 06 Needs — Scored

| # | Statement (abbreviated) | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under? | Over? | Impact | prod_rel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Calibration drift <±0.5% / 12mo | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | YES | no | reliability | false |
| 2 | Glycol concentration accuracy 25–40% | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | YES | no | reliability | false |
| 3 | Full operating range 0.5–50 L/min | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | no | no | reliability | true |
| 4 | Air bubble false readings | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | no | no | reliability | true |

| 6 | Pump degradation detection >15% | 8 | 2 | **14.00** | YES | no | reliability | false |
| 7 | Process vs noise >95% classification | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | YES | no | reliability | false |
| 8 | Glycol leak <5% reservoir | 8 | 2 | **14.00** | YES | no | safety | false |
| 9 | Correct flow rate setpoint ±10% | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | no | no | cost | false |
| 10 | Calculate flow from single config | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | no | no | skill | false |
| 11 | Install without drain | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | no | no | time | true |
| 12 | Config error >1% from fluid-type | 8 | 6 | **10.00** | no | no | reliability | true |
| 13 | Verify accuracy in 5 min pre-batch | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | no | no | time | false |
| 14 | False-normal masking blocked jacket | 9 | 2 | **16.00** | YES | no | reliability | false |
| 15 | Isolate anomaly root cause in 5 min | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | YES | no | time | false |
| 16 | Operator expertise for anomaly data | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | no | no | skill | false |
| 17 | Service life >3yr no maintenance | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | no | no | cost | true |

| 19 | No flow path contamination | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | no | no | safety | true |
| 20 | Pressure drop minimization | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | no | no | cost | true |

| PR-5 | Crash-cooling transient error | 6 | 6 | **6.00** | no | no | reliability | true |
| PR-6 | Low-flow S/N degradation | 5 | 5 | **5.00** | no | no | reliability | true |

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Market** | Breweries (NAICS 312120) |
| **Total Needs** | 31 |
| **Underserved Count** | 11 |
| **Overserved Count** | 0 |
| **Avg Opportunity Score** | 10.68 |

### Top 5 Opportunities

| Rank | # | Error Statement | Imp | Sat | Opp | Zone |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 5 | Minimize the time to detect a glycol jacket blockage or partial restriction before fermenter temperature deviates >0.5°C from setpoint | 9 | 2 | **16.00** | Highly Underserved |
| 2 | 14 | Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading that masks a partially blocked glycol jacket | 9 | 2 | **16.00** | Highly Underserved |
| 3 | 24 | Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only after the 2–4 week fermentation cycle has completed, when correction is no longer possible | 9 | 2 | **16.00** | Highly Underserved |
| 4 | 21 | Minimize batch-to-batch variation in fermentation temperature profile caused by uneven glycol distribution across cooling zones (target: <±0.5°C) | 9 | 3 | **15.00** | Underserved |
| 5 | 6 | Minimize the number of undetected glycol pump degradation events that reduce per-zone cooling capacity by >15% | 8 | 2 | **14.00** | Underserved |

**Runners-up at Opp=14:** #8 (glycol leak detection).

### Opportunity Landscape

| Zone | Score Range | Count | % of Total |
|---|---|---|---|
| Highly Underserved | > 15 | 3 | 9.7% |
| Underserved | 12–15 | 8 | 25.8% |
| Appropriately Served | 10–12 | 11 | 35.5% |
| Overserved | < 10 | 9 | 29.0% |

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-Check vs Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
| Metric | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| ODI avg_opportunity_score | 10.68 | This analysis |
| Feature-Fit avg (tech features) | 5.02 | FIT_breweries §09a (mean of 7 features) |

**Cross-check thresholds:**

| Condition | Triggered? | Note |
|---|---|---|
| ODI avg > 12 AND Feature-Fit avg < 4 | **NO** | Feature-Fit avg = 5.02 (above 4) |
| ODI avg < 8 AND Feature-Fit avg > 7 | **NO** | ODI avg = 10.68 (above 8) |

**No inconsistencies detected.** ODI shows significant unmet needs (11 underserved) while Feature-Fit shows moderate technology relevance (5.02 avg). Interpretation: the market has real pain that ultrasonic technology partially addresses — the top opportunities (#5, #14, #24) align with Digital Anomaly Detection (fit=7.00) and No Moving Parts (fit=7.00) which scored highest in Feature-Fit.

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Result | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Formula correct | **PASS** | Spot-checked 5 entries: #5 (9+max(9-2,0)=16), #12 (8+max(8-6,0)=10), #21 (9+max(9-3,0)=15), PR-5 (6+max(6-6,0)=6), PR-7 (5+max(5-6,0)=5). All match. |
| Q2 | Underserved flags correct | **PASS** | 11 entries with imp>7 AND sat<5; verified #1(8,3)=YES, #3(7,5)=NO (7 not >7), #5(9,2)=YES, #12(8,6)=NO (6 not <5), #22(8,4)=YES |
| Q3 | Overserved flags correct | **PASS** | 0 entries with imp<3 AND sat>7; no need has importance <3 |
| Q4 | Need count preserved | **PASS** | 24 (Step 06) + 7 (Step 10) = 31 ≥ 24 (Step 06 count) |
| Q5 | Top-5 ordering correct | **PASS** | 16 > 16 > 16 > 15 > 14 — descending by opp score; ties broken by need number |
| Q6 | Cross-check ran | **PASS** | ODI vs Feature-Fit comparison completed; no inconsistencies |

---

sections.title: Sources
sections.content:
- JTBD_breweries.md (24 error statements, incumbent anchors, alternatives, ODI pre-scores)
- FIT_breweries.md (7 product-related needs from Step 10, 7 tech feature-market fit scores from Step 09)
- 01_product_decomposition.md (constraints generating product-related needs)
- 02_functional_promise.md (commodity FP baseline)
- 05_market_discovery.md (Breweries NAICS 312120 — market context, alternatives)

rows.Strengths: Lowest cost ($50–150), simple installation, no fluid config needed
rows.Weaknesses: Bearing wear 6–18 mo in glycol, ±2–5% drift, no diagnostics, particle jamming

rows.Strengths: ±0.5% accuracy, no moving parts, 100:1 turndown, 4-20 mA output
rows.Weaknesses: 3–5× cost ($800–2,000), 10–20 W power, larger footprint, requires conductive fluid
No flow visibility, no fault detection, late excursion discovery

rows.Recalibration Rationale: PW drifts 2–5% over life (dominant 50–60%); mag stable but minority; manual = zero measurement
No incumbent compensates for glycol concentration changes
Mag handles full range (100:1 turndown); PW limited but functional
PW jams/stalls with air; mag less affected but still errors
No incumbent provides glycol jacket flow monitoring; manual inspection only
No incumbent trends pump degradation; manual checks only
PW has poor S/N (dominant); mag better but no classification logic
No automated leak detection in incumbent brewery glycol setups
Knowledge/engineering need; sensor-independent
Knowledge need; sensor-independent
All inline sensors require system drain for installation
PW needs no fluid config (zero error risk); mag minimal config
PW has no self-diagnostics; mag basic only; mostly skipped in practice
No incumbent detects partial jacket blockage — completely unaddressed

rows.Statement (abbreviated): Isolate anomaly root cause in 5 min
rows.Recalibration Rationale: No incumbent provides diagnostic data for root cause isolation
PW: no diagnostics; mag: complex outputs requiring training
PW bearing life 6–18 mo in glycol dominates; mag >10yr but minority
PW at $50–150 well under target; mag at $800–2,000 exceeds it
PW: some debris risk; mag: clean. Mixed
PW/mag: minimal drop; orifice: high. Mixed
Without per-zone flow monitoring, glycol distribution is uncontrolled
No per-zone flow visibility → starvation undetected until temp drifts
PW: basic pulse only; mag: better data but too expensive to deploy at every point
No flow-based early warning exists; relies on slow-response fermenter temp probe
Mag handles air better; PW jams entirely — mixed
PW may take minutes to restart after air clears
PW is profile-insensitive; mag tolerates shorter runs
DP degrades gradually; PW and ultrasonic lose signal entirely
Mag and PW unaffected by fluid temperature changes to flow reading
PW stalls at low flow (worse); mag excellent at low flow
Purely ultrasonic-specific; mag and PW have no acoustic coupling dependency

rows.Statement (abbreviated): Isolate anomaly root cause in 5 min

rows.Error Statement: Minimize the time to detect a glycol jacket blockage or partial restriction before fermenter temperature deviates >0.5°C from setpoint
Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading that masks a partially blocked glycol jacket
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only after the 2–4 week fermentation cycle has completed, when correction is no longer possible
Minimize batch-to-batch variation in fermentation temperature profile caused by uneven glycol distribution across cooling zones (target: <±0.5°C)
Minimize the number of undetected glycol pump degradation events that reduce per-zone cooling capacity by >15%

rows.Note: 11 entries with imp>7 AND sat<5; verified #1(8,3)=YES, #3(7,5)=NO (7 not >7), #5(9,2)=YES, #12(8,6)=NO (6 not <5), #22(8,4)=YES
0 entries with imp<3 AND sat>7; no need has importance <3
16 > 16 > 16 > 15 > 14 — descending by opp score; ties broken by need number
ODI vs Feature-Fit comparison completed; no inconsistencies

entries.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of undetected calibration drift exceeding ±0.5% of reading over a 12-month service period
Minimize variation in flow measurement accuracy caused by glycol concentration fluctuations between 25% and 40% propylene glycol
Increase the ability to measure flow rate across the full operating range (0.5–50 L/min) without sensor reconfiguration
Minimize the likelihood of false flow readings caused by air bubble entrainment during glycol system refill or maintenance events
Minimize the time to detect a glycol jacket blockage or partial restriction before fermenter temperature deviates >0.5°C from setpoint
Minimize the number of undetected glycol pump degradation events that reduce per-zone cooling capacity by >15%
Increase the ability to distinguish genuine process flow deviations from sensor measurement noise with >95% classification accuracy
Minimize the time to detect a glycol leak in the distribution system before coolant volume loss exceeds 5% of reservoir capacity
Increase the ability to determine the correct glycol flow rate setpoint within ±10% of actual cooling load for each fermenter size and beer style
Increase the ability to calculate required glycol flow rates for different beer styles and fermenter sizes from a single sensor configuration
Minimize the time required to install and commission a flow sensor on an existing glycol line without draining the system
Minimize the likelihood of systematic measurement error >1% caused by incorrect fluid-type configuration at commissioning
Increase the ability to verify sensor accuracy within 5 minutes before starting a fermentation batch
Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading that masks a partially blocked glycol jacket
Increase the ability to isolate flow anomaly root cause (sensor vs glycol pump vs zone valve) within 5 minutes
Minimize the operator expertise required to interpret flow anomaly data and determine the correct corrective action
Increase the sensor service life achievable without scheduled maintenance interventions affecting measurement accuracy (target: >3 years)
Minimize the total installed cost per glycol flow measurement point including sensor, wiring, and power supply (target: <$500)
Minimize the likelihood of sensor-induced flow path contamination requiring line flushing after sensor installation or replacement
Minimize the permanent pressure drop across the measurement point that increases glycol pump energy consumption
Minimize batch-to-batch variation in fermentation temperature profile caused by uneven glycol distribution across cooling zones (target: <±0.5°C)
Increase the ability to cool multiple fermenters simultaneously without flow starvation to any individual cooling zone
Increase the ability to present quantitative cooling system performance data to brewery ownership and quality assurance teams
Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only after the 2–4 week fermentation cycle has completed, when correction is no longer possible
Minimize the likelihood of false flow readings when air entrainment exceeds 2% volume during glycol system refill or valve-switching events
Minimize the time to recover valid flow measurement after transient air entrainment events dissipate from the glycol circuit
Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation from insufficient straight pipe run in space-constrained brewery glycol manifolds with elbows and T-junctions within 5–10 DN upstream
Minimize the duration of measurement blackout caused by partial pipe drainage during glycol circuit maintenance
Minimize the transient measurement error during crash-cooling sequences where glycol temperature changes exceed the compensation algorithm response rate at >2°C/min
Minimize the likelihood of signal-to-noise degradation below the transit-time detection threshold during low-demand glycol flow in small cooling zones
Minimize the risk of acoustic coupling failure when glycol-to-water ratio shifts outside the validated acoustic impedance range due to uncontrolled top-up or evaporation

top_opportunities: Minimize the time to detect a glycol jacket blockage or partial restriction before fermenter temperature deviates >0.5°C from setpoint

Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only after the 2–4 week fermentation cycle has completed, when correction is no longer possible
Minimize batch-to-batch variation in fermentation temperature profile caused by uneven glycol distribution across cooling zones (target: <±0.5°C)
Minimize the number of undetected glycol pump degradation events that reduce per-zone cooling capacity by >15%

properties.full_statement: Increase the ability to cool multiple fermenters simultaneously without flow starvation to any individual cooling zone

key.error_statement: Increase the ability to present quantitative cooling system performance data to b

properties.full_statement: Increase the ability to present quantitative cooling system performance data to brewery ownership and quality assurance teams

key.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only

properties.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only after the 2–4 week fermentation cycle has completed, when correction is no longer possible

key.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of false flow readings when air entrainment exceeds 2% vo

properties.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of false flow readings when air entrainment exceeds 2% volume during glycol system refill or valve-switching events

key.error_statement: Minimize the time to recover valid flow measurement after transient air entrainme

properties.full_statement: Minimize the time to recover valid flow measurement after transient air entrainment events dissipate from the glycol circuit

key.error_statement: Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation from insufficient straight pipe run

properties.full_statement: Minimize the measurement accuracy degradation from insufficient straight pipe run in space-constrained brewery glycol manifolds with elbows and T-junctions within 5–10 DN upstream

key.error_statement: Minimize the duration of measurement blackout caused by partial pipe drainage dur

properties.full_statement: Minimize the duration of measurement blackout caused by partial pipe drainage during glycol circuit maintenance

key.error_statement: Minimize the transient measurement error during crash-cooling sequences where gly

properties.full_statement: Minimize the transient measurement error during crash-cooling sequences where glycol temperature changes exceed the compensation algorithm response rate at >2°C/min

key.error_statement: Minimize the likelihood of signal-to-noise degradation below the transit-time det

properties.full_statement: Minimize the likelihood of signal-to-noise degradation below the transit-time detection threshold during low-demand glycol flow in small cooling zones

key.error_statement: Minimize the risk of acoustic coupling failure when glycol-to-water ratio shifts

properties.full_statement: Minimize the risk of acoustic coupling failure when glycol-to-water ratio shifts outside the validated acoustic impedance range due to uncontrolled top-up or evaporation

market_odi_props.odi_top_opportunities: ["Minimize the time to detect a glycol jacket blockage or partial restriction before fermenter temperature deviates >0.5°C from setpoint","Minimize the likelihood of accepting a false-normal flow reading that masks a partially blocked glycol jacket","Minimize the likelihood of discovering a fermentation temperature excursion only after the 2–4 week fermentation cycle has completed, when correction is no longer possible","Minimize batch-to-batch variation in fermentation temperature profile caused by uneven glycol distribution across cooling zones (target: <±0.5°C)","Minimize the number of undetected glycol pump degradation events that reduce per-zone cooling capacity by >15%"]
